source_article.title_en: Fantasy Baseball Week 4 Preview: Top 10 sleeper pitchers feature Ryan Weathers, Randy Vasquez
source_article.author: Scott White
source_article.dek_en: Target top fantasy baseball streamers like Weathers, Nelson and Springs with favorable matchups and upside
source_article.body_en:
Streaming pitchers isn't for the faint of heart, but if you find yourself without enough reliable options in a given scoring period, it may be what you need to stay afloat. Scott White has 10 recommendations for the upcoming scoring period, all rostered in less than 80 percent of CBS Sports leagues. Generally, only the top two or three are full-throated endorsements, while the rest are more about making the best of a bad situation.

Be sure to check back Sunday for the latest updates.

1. Ryan Weathers (SP) - Ryan Weathers took a little off his pitches to focus on better strike-throwing in his latest outing and ended up delivering an eight-inning gem against the Athletics. He has two favorable matchups this week, particularly the first against the most strikeout-prone lineup in baseball.
2. Braxton Ashcraft (P) - Braxton Ashcraft is a ground-ball pitcher who misses bats better than most, which gives him a high floor. His matchup against the Nationals this week gives him a high ceiling.
3. Ryne Nelson (SP) - Ryne Nelson righted the ship against a tough Mets lineup in his most recent start, which makes me less fearful of his tough matchups this week. A pitcher with a 3.33 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, and 8.0 K/9 over his past 251 2/3 innings is basically a must when he's in line for two starts.
4. Grant Holmes (RP) - After three starts, Grant Holmes has already put to rest the concerns about his elbow coming into the season, and he should enjoy an uptick in strikeouts soon enough, given that his slider is sporting a 50 percent whiff rate. His second matchup against the Phillies could be tough, but you'll take two however you can get them.
5. Joey Cantillo (SP) - Joey Cantillo hasn't been around as long as Holmes, but he profiles similarly as a quality bat-misser whose outings will tend to run short and WHIP will tend to run high. Also like Holmes, he has one good matchup and one bad one this week.
6. Jeffrey Springs (SP) - Jeffrey Springs' fastball is up only a little this year, but judging by the early results, it seems to have made a world of difference. He's allowed a combined three hits in his past two starts, striking out 13 over 13 innings, and has two inviting matchups this week.
7. Mitch Keller (SP) - Though he's been effective so far, Mitch Keller presents a ton of ERA and WHIP risk for categories leagues (and without much strikeout upside), which could obviously be doubly damaging in a two-start week. This recommendation is more for points leagues, where such ratio stats aren't scored directly, and Keller's knack for pitching deep into games is of more value.
8. Will Warren (P) - Though Will Warren hasn't been bad, per se, two of his three starts have fallen short of the five-inning mark that's the minimum threshold for a pitcher to matter in Fantasy. He'll get two bites at the apple this week, though, and both of his matchups are favorable.
9. Randy Vasquez (RP) - The Mariners lineup is off to a rough start, and part of that is having the second-most strikeouts of any team. Randy Vasquez has been a surprising source of strikeouts early on, thanks to a fastball that's up about 1 mph and features an additional inch of induced vertical break.
10. Michael Burrows (SP) - Michael Burrows has been pretty bad so far, even with a couple favorable matchups, but I still have faith in the Astros' plan for him. I might roll the dice in a two-start week, particularly with one being against an ice cold Mariners lineup.

player_cards.blurb_en: Ryan Weathers took a little off his pitches to focus on better strike-throwing in his latest outing and ended up delivering an eight-inning gem against the Athletics. He has two favorable matchups this week, particularly the first against the most strikeout-prone lineup in baseball.
Braxton Ashcraft is a ground-ball pitcher who misses bats better than most, which gives him a high floor. His matchup against the Nationals this week gives him a high ceiling.

Ryne Nelson righted the ship against a tough Mets lineup in his most recent start, which makes me less fearful of his tough matchups this week. A pitcher with a 3.33 ERA, 1.05 WHIP, and 8.0 K/9 over his past 251 2/3 innings is basically a must when he's in line for two starts.

After three starts, Grant Holmes has already put to rest the concerns about his elbow coming into the season, and he should enjoy an uptick in strikeouts soon enough, given that his slider is sporting a 50 percent whiff rate. His second matchup against the Phillies could be tough, but you'll take two however you can get them.

Joey Cantillo hasn't been around as long as Holmes, but he profiles similarly as a quality bat-misser whose outings will tend to run short and WHIP will tend to run high. Also like Holmes, he has one good matchup and one bad one this week.

Jeffrey Springs' fastball is up only a little this year, but judging by the early results, it seems to have made a world of difference. He's allowed a combined three hits in his past two starts, striking out 13 over 13 innings, and has two inviting matchups this week.

Though he's been effective so far, Mitch Keller presents a ton of ERA and WHIP risk for categories leagues (and without much strikeout upside), which could obviously be doubly damaging in a two-start week. This recommendation is more for points leagues, where such ratio stats aren't scored directly, and Keller's knack for pitching deep into games is of more value.

Though Will Warren hasn't been bad, per se, two of his three starts have fallen short of the five-inning mark that's the minimum threshold for a pitcher to matter in Fantasy. He'll get two bites at the apple this week, though, and both of his matchups are favorable.

The Mariners lineup is off to a rough start, and part of that is having the second-most strikeouts of any team. Randy Vasquez has been a surprising source of strikeouts early on, thanks to a fastball that's up about 1 mph and features an additional inch of induced vertical break.

Michael Burrows has been pretty bad so far, even with a couple favorable matchups, but I still have faith in the Astros' plan for him. I might roll the dice in a two-start week, particularly with one being against an ice cold Mariners lineup.

ai_article.article_summary_zh_tw: 本週的先發投手睡眠名單聚焦於具兩場先發或對戰有利的投手，頭號推薦包括 Ryan Weathers、Ryne Nelson 與 Jeffrey Springs 等人。

players.summary_en: He has two favorable matchups this week, particularly the first against the most strikeout-prone lineup in baseball.
players.summary_zh_tw: Ryan Weathers 本週有兩個有利對戰，尤其首場面對全聯盟最容易三振的打線。

players.summary_en: He is a ground-ball pitcher who misses bats better than most, and his matchup against the Nationals gives him a high ceiling.
players.summary_zh_tw: Braxton Ashcraft 是具滾地球能力且能製造揮空的投手，而對國民的對戰讓他上限很高。

players.summary_en: He has tough matchups this week, but a recent strong outing and a solid run of ratios over his past 251 2/3 innings make him attractive in a two-start week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Ryne Nelson 雖然本週對戰不易，但近期穩定的成績讓他在兩場先發週仍具吸引力。

players.summary_en: He has already quieted elbow concerns and should see more strikeouts soon, though his second matchup against the Phillies could be tough.
players.summary_zh_tw: Grant Holmes 已經消除了手肘疑慮且有望提升三振數，但他對費城人的第二場對戰可能偏硬。

players.summary_en: He profiles as a quality bat-misser, but his outings may be short and his WHIP may run high, with one good matchup and one bad one this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Joey Cantillo 具備不錯的製造揮空能力，但投球局數可能偏短且 WHIP 有風險，且本週是一好一壞的對戰組合。

players.summary_en: His slightly elevated fastball has made a big difference, as he has allowed three hits and struck out 13 over his past two starts, with two inviting matchups ahead.
players.summary_zh_tw: Jeffrey Springs 的速球些微上調後效果明顯，近兩戰僅失三安並送出十三次三振，而且本週還有兩場理想對戰。

players.summary_en: He has plenty of ERA and WHIP risk with limited strikeout upside, making him more useful in points leagues than in categories formats.
players.summary_zh_tw: Mitch Keller 雖然表現有效率，但 ERA 與 WHIP 風險偏高且三振上限有限，因此在積分制聯盟比類別制更實用。

players.summary_en: He has not consistently reached five innings, but he gets two favorable matchups this week.
players.summary_zh_tw: Will Warren 雖然未必能穩定投滿五局，但本週仍有兩場有利對戰。

players.summary_en: The Mariners are striking out a lot, and he has been a surprising source of strikeouts early thanks to improved fastball traits.
players.summary_zh_tw: Randy Vasquez 面對近期大量三振的水手隊，加上速球特性提升，讓他成為意外的三振來源。

players.summary_en: He has struggled so far, but there is still faith in the Astros' plan for him and a two-start week could be worth the gamble.
players.summary_zh_tw: Michael Burrows 目前表現不佳，但火箭隊對他的培養計畫仍受看好，而兩場先發週值得冒險一試。
